methodology: Ratification reach for rights-expansion and protection treaties. Number of state parties reflects status from the UN Treaty Collection. The most-ratified treaties show what humanity has formally agreed on, even where compliance falls short. The Ledger tracks who refuses; The Lift tracks what got broadly signed.
data_status: illustrative — reflects public ratification status as of last verification

treaties.name: Convention on the Rights of the Child
treaties.scope: Most widely ratified human rights treaty in history
treaties.notes: Every UN member state except the United States has ratified. Establishes a child-rights floor governments can be measured against.

treaties.name: Geneva Conventions (I–IV)
treaties.scope: Laws of war; protection of wounded, sick, prisoners, civilians
treaties.notes: Universal ratification. Compliance is uneven; the standard itself is the most-agreed-upon body of conduct rules in history.

treaties.name: Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women
treaties.notes: Near-universal but with substantive reservations from many state parties. Reporting cycle is the active accountability mechanism.

treaties.name: International Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination
treaties.notes: One of the earliest core UN rights treaties; Article 4 hate-speech provisions remain contested in some Western jurisdictions.

treaties.name: WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control
treaties.scope: First treaty negotiated under WHO; tobacco demand- and supply-reduction measures
treaties.notes: Credited with rapid global declines in adult smoking prevalence; structural rather than behavioral intervention.

treaties.name: Marrakesh Treaty (accessible books)
treaties.scope: Mandatory copyright exceptions for blind, visually impaired, and print-disabled readers
treaties.notes: Pioneered the principle that copyright cannot block access for the print-disabled. Cross-border exchange of accessible books is now legal.

treaties.name: Minamata Convention on Mercury
treaties.scope: Protects human health and environment from anthropogenic mercury emissions
treaties.notes: Phase-out timetable for many mercury products; small-scale gold mining is the harder unfinished file.

treaties.name: Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer
treaties.scope: First universally ratified UN treaty; phase-out of CFCs and HCFCs
treaties.notes: Ozone layer projected to recover to 1980 levels by 2066 over most of the globe. Kigali Amendment (2016) added HFCs.

treaties.name: Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework
treaties.scope: 30% of land and ocean protected by 2030; restoration of degraded ecosystems
treaties.notes: Adopted under the Convention on Biological Diversity (which itself has 196 parties; US is the principal non-party). Implementation is the next test.
